l1: Question 1 — How are Printers pushed on the client machines?GPO/GPP/point and print or third party
Question 2 - Print Client OS and Patch information?
Question 3 - Print Server OS and Patch information?
Question 4 - Issue Description and Error message if any?
Question 5 - How many Printers affected?
Question 6 - How many Print Clients Affected?
l2: What are the events logged for this issue on the event logs.?
Is the print spooler service running and have you tried rebooting the print spooler service or not?
Have you tried pinging the printers ip address?
Have you tried changing the print driver for the printer (or updated the driver)
Collect the Print Driver Information.What is the exact error message?

l1: Ouestion 1 - What is the error message?
Question 2 - Is only a single user affected or others as well?
Question 3 - Are we able to print directly from server?
Question 4 - How the printers are added/installed to the machine?
Question 5 - Was it working correctly before?
Question 6 - What was the last change made on server/client?
l2: Issue is with all the printer or with any specific printer? Collect printer make and model with driver version?
If there is SAP printing then whether its only SAP printing is affected?
Did customer engage SAP, If no then ask for engagement. Their insight may help to understand the behaviour change from Vendor Perspective
What is the type of printer on which you are facing the issue? Local / Network?
Check the behavior after making RestrictedDriverInstallationtoAdministrator to 0?

l1: Check the error message?
Check the print spooler service and check the dependencies
Check the connectivity of the printer from the machine
Orphaned jobs in print spooler folder c:/windows/system32/spool/printers
In the case of client server scenario, check if the printing is working on the server
Print a test page from the printer itself (device specific issue)
Update the printer drier to the latest version
Check if the clipboard redirection is working or not (copy paste)
Check for any group policy restricting the printer redirection ( Do not allow printer redirection)
Run the sec /scannow command
Check for the Remote Desktop device redirector bus in device manager
Check for the UMRDP (Remote Desktop Services UserMode Port Redirector) service is it on or off
l2: Update the printing binaries to the latest version
Change the print driver to “generic text” and then test.
Uncheck all the advanced options from the printer properties
Check the Print service logs in event viewer.
Check the permissions on the spooler folder
Check the Application logs for the event id 1000 for the crashing issue, check the faulting module mentioned in that event
If faulting module is detected/mentioned research for the dll and if the driver was identified change/update the driver
Search for the dll file in the c:/windows and the registry, rename the dll name to .old
Print drivers isolation (Only after discussion)

Check for the redirection settings on the Listener (Registry and the listener 2008R2) setting on the server (HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Control/Terminal Server/Wds/rdpwd/fDisableCpm set to 1)(HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Control/Terminal Server)(HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Control/Terminal Services/fDisableCpm set to 1)

Group Policy must be correctly set to enable Easy Print on the Server. The policy location is “Computer Configuration -> Administrative templates -> Windows Component -> Remote Desktop Services -> Remote Desktop Session Host -> Printer Redirection”. The setting “Use Remote DESKTOP Easy Print printer driver first” must be set to “Enabled” for Easy Print redirection, and it has to be “Disabled”
For Legacy Print. For “Not Configured”, Easy Print is chosen by Default.
If the user is using an RDP file check if the following value is set “redirectprinters:i:1”
Check the tsprint binaries and update them

l1: Export System and Application event logs in .evtx format.
Export Print Service Operation Logs in .evtx format
Collect gpresult.
l2: Collect procmon at the time of reproducing the issue
Collect TSS for Printing
Capture a network trace

printing_error_0x709.title: Unable to connect to printer getting error 0x00000709 (Operation could not be completed)

steps.action: This issue may occur if certain non Microsoft DNS solutions are providing name resolution for the network/Clients try to connect to the printers by using the CNAME record in the UNC path
steps.expect: Operation could ot be completed (error 0x00000709) Double chec the printer name and make sure that the printer is connected to the network

steps.action: Check if the print server has been changed or there must be change in the print server nane
steps.expect: There must be new print server in the environment or DNS enry has been changed

steps.action: Implement the DnsOnWire registry value by running the below command (reg add HKLM/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Control/Print /v DnsOnWire /t REG_DWORD /d 1)/(Edit the local Hosts file to include the CNAME record for the server.)
steps.expect: Restart the Print Spooler Service on Print Server

steps.action: Try to add the printer on the cliet machine using the UNC path
steps.expect: User will be able to connect to the printer
